html:
On a recent episode of the eCommerce Podcast with Matt Edmundson, Revolv3's Founder and CEO, Frank Arellano, unpacked one of the most costly and overlooked issues in payments and e-commerce: false declines, legitimate transactions that get wrongly rejected.

These silent killers account for up to two-thirds of all credit card declines, contributing to an estimated $600 billion in lost revenue in 2024 alone. For subscription-based businesses, the impact is even more severe, leading to involuntary churn, customers lost not by choice, but due to failed payments they never saw coming.

Why False Declines Happen

The payment ecosystem is complex: merchants, gateways, processors, card networks, and issuing banks all apply different fraud rules. Without coordination, valid transactions get caught in the crossfire. And when that happens, customers blame the business, not the bank.

How Revolv3 Is Changing the Game

At Revolv3, we’re rethinking the payment ecosystem. Our intelligent optimization and orchestration platform analyzes over 100 data points per transaction to build trust with issuers and increase approval rates. Unlike traditional gateways, we focus on data quality, timing, and issuer-specific preferences to ensure more transactions go through. This leads to a 14% average increase in approvals for our customers and up to 30% revenue growth from better management of the entire payment process.

Beyond the Numbers: A Vision for the Future

With over 25 years of experience at companies like Ingram Micro and Experian, Frank brings a holistic view to the future of payments. He sees embedded finance, predictive analytics, and tokenization as the next frontiers, alongside growing importance in regulatory compliance.

His advice for fintech founders?  

“Understand how money moves throughout the entire ecosystem—not just one part of it.”

What You Can Do Today

If you're running a subscription or eCommerce business, here are three steps to start reducing false declines:

  1. Audit your approval rates – Know your numbers.
  1. Offer multiple payment methods – Give customers options.
  1. Optimize your payment stack – Platforms like Revolv3 can help you recover lost revenue without new marketing spend.

False declines aren’t just a technical issue, they’re a growth opportunity. At Revolv3, we’re helping businesses turn payment friction into profit. You can book a demo with us to get started and check out the eCommerce podcast episode below to dive deeper into your payments and improve your business.
